Index.title: Software Engineer & Builder
Index.description: Looking to advance the frontier of science and technology. I have studied AI, science, and technology to build systems that scale.

project1.title: Autonomous Infrastructure
project1.description: Developing self-healing environments using large action models to minimize operational latency.

project2.title: Deterministic Agents
project2.description: A framework for LLM orchestration that guarantees verifiable output paths through symbolic logic constraints.

project3.title: Modular OS
project3.description: Redefining the personal workspace as a composable set of technical primitives for deep work and high output.

project1.description: A portable on-chain record of hackathon participation, contribution, and achievements. It enables builders to establish a unified identity, receive official awards, and facilitate peer attestation on the Monad network.

project2.description: A Kraken-native low-code strategy builder. Allows traders to visually compose logic routines, execute them safely in a rigorous dry-run environment, and optionally deploy them for live algorithmic trading.

principle1.title: Deterministic Systems
principle1.description: Complexity should never breed unpredictability. I build systems where the outcome is a mathematical certainty, not a statistical probability.

principle2.title: Minimalist Engineering
principle2.description: Perfection is achieved not when there is nothing more to add, but when there is nothing left to take away from the codebase.

principle3.title: Agency as a Partner
principle3.description: AI isn't a replacement for the builder, but a high-fidelity partner that extends our cognitive reach across vast technical surfaces.

item1.label: AGE 20 · PRESENT

contact.label: Ready to Build?

Projects.title: Projects
Projects.description: Systems I've built and the repositories behind them. A living directory, updated as I ship.

builder_passport.name: Builder Passport
builder_passport.description: A portable on-chain record of hackathon participation, contribution, and achievements. It enables builders to establish a unified identity, receive official awards, and facilitate peer attestation on the Monad network.

kraken_dad.name: Kraken DaD
kraken_dad.description: A Kraken-native low-code strategy builder. Allows traders to visually compose logic routines, execute them safely in a rigorous dry-run environment, and optionally deploy them for live algorithmic trading.

bedside.name: Bedside
bedside.description: An AI inpatient assistant delivered via WhatsApp. Patients text to get their schedule, medications, and reach clinical staff in their own language. Multi-tenant SaaS for hospitals — built for the Harvard HSIL Hackathon 2026.

pitch_maker.name: Pitch Maker
pitch_maker.description: An AI-powered tool that generates cinematic pitch videos. Automatically creates scripts, professional voiceovers, and stunning visuals from a short project spec — compressing a week of production into minutes.

sealship.name: Sealship
sealship.description: A decentralized developer identity and code reputation system built for the Polkadot Solidity Hackathon 2026. Analyzes GitHub repos to produce a structured quality score, generates AI-powered reports, stores them on IPFS, and records cryptographic verification on-chain. Seal your code. Ship your trust.

biome.name: Biome
biome.description: Biometric Optimization Management Engine — a personal system for collecting, tracking, and acting on biometric signals. Focused on turning raw body data into clear, day-to-day optimization loops.

Journey.title: Journey
Journey.intro: I'm Patrick Passos — 20 years old, born and raised in Brazil. This page is the real story of how I went from writing my first line of code at twelve to shipping at hackathons today, told in order.

age_12.label: AGE 12
age_12.title: First Logic & Games
age_12.description: I enrolled in a programming course focused on game development and built my first small games. Seeing an idea turn into working software felt like magic, and that early problem-solving set the direction for everything that followed.

age_17.label: AGE 17
age_17.title: Technical High School
age_17.description: I joined a technical high school specializing in informatics to pair a real computer science foundation with my secondary education. Coursework was useful, but I quickly learned that serious engineers have to build their own curriculum.

age_18.label: AGE 18
age_18.title: Full English Immersion
age_18.description: I committed to fluency through total immersion — switching every device (phone, computer, TV) to English, reading constantly, and launching a YouTube channel to practice on camera every day. English stopped being a class and became the operating language of my life.

age_19_canada.label: AGE 19 · CANADA
age_19_canada.title: Exchange Program
age_19_canada.description: I flew to Canada for an exchange program to put my English under real pressure and widen my lens on the world. Living abroad rewired how I think about work, ambition, and what's actually possible.

age_19_sf.label: AGE 19 · SAN FRANCISCO
age_19_sf.title: Silicon Valley
age_19_sf.description: From Canada I made my way to San Francisco to stand inside the engine room of the global technology scene. Being close to founders and builders in the Bay showed me, up close, what software can do when it's pointed at real problems.

age_20_present.label: AGE 20 · PRESENT
age_20_present.title: Hackathons & Building
age_20_present.description: Back in Brazil, I went straight into the hackathon scene, starting with the NASA Space Apps Challenge — the largest hackathon in the world. Since then I've been shipping at hackathons (Suno AI, Agents Hackathon, and more), building projects like Builder Passport and Kraken DaD, and working toward software that solves real problems with talented teams.

Services.label: Freelance — Open for New Projects
Services.title: Services
Services.description: I build back-end, front-end, full systems, and AI automations with code. Fast to ramp on new stacks — I pair with Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode to ship in unfamiliar territory.

offer.title: What I Build
offer.subtitle: End-to-end delivery, tailored to the problem.

item1.label: BACK-END
item1.title: APIs & Services
item1.description: Services, APIs, and data pipelines in Node.js, Python (FastAPI), and TypeScript. Built to be deterministic and observable.

item2.label: FRONT-END
item2.title: Web Interfaces
item2.description: Production React and Next.js interfaces. Typed, accessible, and fast — with honest attention to UX.

item3.label: FULL SYSTEMS
item3.title: End-to-End Applications
item3.description: Complete applications wired end-to-end — database, API, UI, auth, deploy. Shipping the whole slice, not just pieces.

item4.label: AI AUTOMATIONS
item4.title: Agent Workflows
item4.description: Agent workflows and automations using Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode. Replacing repetitive work with reliable, auditable pipelines.

item1.title: AI-native shipping
item1.description: I pair with Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode on every project. That means faster iteration without sacrificing review or correctness.

item2.title: Fast to ramp
item2.description: New stack or unfamiliar domain? I ramp quickly — reading docs, experimenting, and using agents as leverage to cover ground that would otherwise take weeks.

item3.title: Ship, then refine
item3.description: I deliver the smallest real thing that proves the idea, then refine against feedback. Working software beats a long plan.

projects_cta.title: See my work
projects_cta.description: A living directory of systems I've shipped — from hackathon builds to on-chain systems.

contact.label: Open to Freelance Work
contact.title: Let's build something.
contact.description: Have a project in mind? Reach out directly or book a call — I respond within 24 hours.
